scenario.context: Station 01: Onboarding wave with multiple follow-up tasks on role, rights and team behavior.
scenario.mission: You handle the initial task and several follow-up tasks along the first training day with increasing responsibility.

station.profile: Onboarding, role understanding, rights and duties, team communication
constraints: keep role clarity as an apprentice

customerRequest.subject: Station 01: First day at the company with follow-up tasks
customerRequest.body:
Please work on several tasks for the first day at the training company that build on each other.

Focus: stakeholders in the dual system, role in the team, rights and duties, co-determination, career planning and a clean day closure.

questions.title: Classify learning locations correctly
questions.prompt: Which combination best describes the dual system?

options.text: Only vocational school and self-study
options.rationale: The company as a central learning location is missing.

options.text: Training company and vocational school, supported by responsible institutions
options.rationale: That correctly reflects the dual structure.

options.text: Only the training company and the exam day
options.rationale: Vocational school and ongoing support are missing.

feedback.correct: Correct: company and vocational school are the core learning locations.
feedback.wrong: Incomplete: please classify the dual structure correctly.

questions.title: Stakeholders and roles
questions.prompt: Which statements about stakeholders in the dual system are correct? (Multiple selection)

options.text: Trainers provide professional guidance and feedback
options.rationale: This is part of the core training task.

options.text: Vocational school teaches the theoretical foundations
options.rationale: School and company are intertwined in content.

options.text: Apprentices do not need to ask questions
options.rationale: Questions are part of professional learning.

options.text: Responsible institutions help secure the legal framework
options.rationale: They are part of the overall structure.

options.text: Team colleagues have no influence on learning progress
options.rationale: Practical learning in the team is central.

feedback.correct: Correct: the roles are properly classified.
feedback.wrong: Partially wrong: please separate learning locations and responsibilities more clearly.

questions.title: Start sequence on the first day
questions.prompt: Put the sequence in order for a professional start at the company.
items: Take on tasks with expectation clarification
Get to know the team and responsibilities
Go through workplace and safety rules
Note open questions and learning points
correctOrder: Get to know the team and responsibilities
Go through workplace and safety rules
Take on tasks with expectation clarification
Note open questions and learning points

feedback.correct: Correct: this cleanly combines orientation, work and reflection.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: please start with orientation, then tasks and reflection.

questions.title: Your role in the team
questions.prompt: Describe in 4-6 sentences your role as an apprentice in the IT team.
questions.expectedOutline: Role between learning and contributing, clear responsibility boundaries, active question behavior.

feedback.correct: Correct: the role description is reflective and practical.
feedback.wrong: Please elaborate learning task, responsibility and questions more clearly.

questions.title: Calculate onboarding time
questions.prompt: Four introductory blocks take 35 minutes each, plus 30 minutes of team round. How many minutes should be planned in total?

feedback.correct: Correct calculation.
feedback.wrong: Please add block times and team round correctly.

questions.title: Follow-up task 1 - Rights and duties in daily work
questions.subject: Reporting paths, diligence and accountability
questions.body: Please review typical daily situations regarding rights and duties and make justified decisions.

questions.title: Duties in training daily work
questions.prompt: Which behaviors comply with duties? (Multiple selection)

options.text: Handle work assignments carefully and document them
options.rationale: Diligence and traceability are part of it.

options.text: Provide early feedback when issues arise
Suspend safety rules under time pressure
Actively use learning opportunities
Skip documentation when stressed

feedback.correct: Correct: this shows a professional understanding of duties.
feedback.wrong: Partially wrong: please weigh accountability and safety more strongly.

questions.title: Uncertainty about a task
questions.prompt: How do you respond professionally when you are not yet confident about a task?

options.text: Ask a question, clarify the goal and align the implementation
options.rationale: This reduces errors and shows professionalism.

options.text: Submit something without coordination
Silently ignore the task

feedback.correct: Correct: clarifying the goal and aligning are the right path.
feedback.wrong: Not appropriate: when unsure, transparency and consultation are required.

questions.title: Formulate a critical clarification question
questions.prompt: Formulate a short clarification question (4-6 sentences) when a task is unclear and time-critical.
questions.expectedOutline: State the issue factually, ask a concrete question, offer a practical proposal.

feedback.correct: Correct: the question is professional and solution-oriented.
feedback.wrong: Please separate task, uncertainty and proposal more clearly.

questions.title: Secure the reporting chain in time
questions.prompt: An incident must be reported internally within 20 minutes. 6 minutes pass for analysis, 4 for documentation. How many minutes remain for the report?

feedback.correct: Correct calculation.
feedback.wrong: Please calculate the remaining time after analysis and documentation correctly.

questions.title: Forward an incident correctly
questions.prompt: Order the sequence for a clean report in an operational incident.
items: Send the report to the responsible unit
Document the incident in a structured way
correctOrder: Document the incident in a structured way
Send the report to the responsible unit

feedback.correct: Correct: the sequence is traceable and reliable.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: please sequence verification, documentation and reporting cleanly.

questions.title: Follow-up task 2 - Labor rights and co-determination
questions.subject: Address concerns correctly and use escalation paths
questions.body: Please handle situations around co-determination, conflict communication and proper escalation in daily team work.

questions.title: Place a concern correctly
questions.prompt: A team process affects several colleagues negatively on a recurring basis. What is the best first step?

options.text: Prepare the concern factually and submit it through the designated co-determination channel
Ignore the problem as long as no customer is affected
Escalate publicly immediately without prior clarification

feedback.correct: Correct: factual, appropriate channel selection is professional.
feedback.wrong: Not appropriate: concerns need an orderly path.

questions.title: Co-determination in practice
questions.prompt: Which points support effective co-determination in the company? (Multiple selection)

options.text: Prepare topics with robust examples
Show impacts on team and processes
Handle conflicts only personally and without documentation
options.rationale: Without evidence there is no commitment.

options.text: Name responsible persons and deadlines clearly
Ignore feedback when it is critical
options.rationale: Learning and improvement opportunities are lost.

feedback.correct: Correct: this promotes reliable co-determination.
feedback.wrong: Partially wrong: please include procedural clarity and traceability more strongly.

questions.title: Structure a conflict conversation
questions.prompt: Order the sequence for a professional conflict conversation.

feedback.correct: Correct: objectivity and commitment are well combined.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: please clarify first, then solve and formalize.

questions.title: Write factual feedback
questions.prompt: Formulate factual feedback (4-6 sentences) on an unclear team rule.
questions.expectedOutline: Briefly name situation and impact, make a practical proposal.

feedback.correct: Correct: the feedback is professional and constructive.
feedback.wrong: Please separate situation, impact and proposal more clearly.

questions.title: Time window for clarification
questions.prompt: A clarification meeting is planned for 45 minutes. 18 minutes are already used for describing the facts and 12 minutes for questions. How much time remains for agreeing on a solution?

feedback.correct: Correct calculation.
feedback.wrong: Please calculate the remaining time correctly.

questions.title: Follow-up task 3 - Career and life planning
questions.subject: Plan development goals and learning time in a structured way
questions.body: Please create a realistic development framework for the next months and prioritize learning goals in a traceable way.

questions.title: Elements of a development plan
questions.prompt: Which elements belong in a solid career and learning plan? (Multiple selection)

options.text: Clear learning goals with a timeframe
Practical tasks to apply what was learned
Only random tasks without goal alignment
options.rationale: Without a goal, there is no development line.

options.text: Regular reflection points with feedback
Filter out critical feedback

feedback.correct: Correct: this is a sustainable development basis.
feedback.wrong: Partially wrong: please link goal alignment, transfer and reflection more strongly.

questions.title: Next development step
questions.prompt: Which next step makes the most sense after a week of onboarding?

options.text: Align a concrete learning goal with a practical task and a date
Wait until a suitable task appears by chance
Collect only theoretical topics without application
options.rationale: Transfer to the company is missing.

feedback.correct: Correct: goal, task and date make development manageable.
feedback.wrong: Not appropriate: the step must be concrete and actionable.

questions.title: Development plan in brief
questions.prompt: Describe in 4-6 sentences a development plan for the next 6 weeks.
questions.expectedOutline: Concrete goal picture, practical application, feedback and check points.

feedback.correct: Correct: the plan is realistic and learning-effective.
feedback.wrong: Please elaborate goal, practical share and feedback mechanism more clearly.

questions.title: Learning time per month
questions.prompt: Planned learning time is 3.5 hours per week. How many hours should be planned in 6 weeks?

feedback.correct: Correct calculation.
feedback.wrong: Please multiply the weekly amount by the number of weeks.

questions.title: Build a learning goal process
questions.prompt: Order the steps from learning goal to evaluation.

feedback.correct: Correct: the learning process is didactically well structured.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: please structure from goal via practice to evaluation.

questions.title: Follow-up task 4 - End-of-day wrap-up and team handover
questions.subject: Status, open items and next step by end of day
questions.body: The day's results should be clearly documented and handed over to the team. Please prioritize transparency and traceability.

questions.title: Short status at end of day
questions.prompt: Which statement best fits a professional end-of-day status?

options.text: Only report that everything is roughly done
Name completed items, open risks and next action concretely
options.rationale: This is directly usable for teamwork.

options.text: Only refer to tomorrow without any result reference

feedback.correct: Correct: this makes the status handover-ready.
feedback.wrong: Not appropriate: a status needs result, risk and next action.

questions.title: Documentation for team handover
questions.prompt: Which contents belong in a good team handover? (Multiple selection)

options.text: Short result status per work package
Open items with responsible persons
Unsorted notes without context
Risk or blocker notes
Personal opinions without factual basis

feedback.correct: Correct: the handover is clear and connectable.
feedback.wrong: Partially wrong: please structure facts, responsibilities and risks more strongly.

questions.title: Structured end of day
questions.prompt: Order the steps for a professional end-of-day wrap-up.
items: Send a short status to the team
File documents in an orderly way
correctOrder: Send a short status to the team
File documents in an orderly way

feedback.correct: Correct: this is a clean closing process.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: please document and plan first, then communicate.

questions.title: Write a closing message
questions.prompt: Write a closing message (5-7 sentences) with status, open item, risk and next step.
questions.expectedOutline: Clear status, open items with risk, concrete follow-up action.

feedback.correct: Correct: the message is team-ready and decision-capable.
feedback.wrong: Please phrase status, open item and next step more concretely.
